name: Ansar-Ud-Deen Grammar School, Surulere
description: Private day and boarding boys' secondary school founded in 1958, run under the Ansar-Ud-Deen Education Trust (ADET) of the Ansar-Ud-Deen Society of Nigeria; emphasises high quality academic, moral and religious education.
evidence: ADET (Ansar-Ud-Deen Education Trust) official site lists the school with address, phones, email and 1958 founding.

name: Ansar-Ud-Deen College, Isolo
description: Co-educational secondary school founded in 1953, one of three schools run by the Ansar-Ud-Deen Education Trust (ADET) of the Ansar-Ud-Deen Society; described as a lively, warm community encouraging academic and technical excellence.
address: 1 Akinbaiye Street, off Mushin Road, Isolo, Lagos
evidence: ADET official site (adetschools.org) states founding 1953, Isolo address, phones and email.

name: Ansar-Ud-Deen Girls' High School, Itire
description: Girls' secondary school founded 22 January 1960 by the Ansar-Ud-Deen Society of Nigeria, run under the Ansar-Ud-Deen Education Trust (ADET); motto 'Centre of Rich Professional Excellence'.
evidence: ADET official site page for the school gives address, phones, email, 1960 founding and Ansar-Ud-Deen Society ownership.

name: Ansar-Ud-Deen Society Senior Grammar School, Badagry
description: Public senior secondary school established 1 September 1980, founded by the Ansar-Ud-Deen Society; offers 21 senior secondary subjects with science laboratories, library and playing field.
evidence: Nigerian Schools Directory (erudites.ng) listing gives name, Badagry address, 1980 establishment date and photo.

name: Ansar-Ud-Deen Society Junior Grammar School, Badagry
description: Public junior secondary school established 6 January 2003 on the Ansar-Ud-Deen Society school compound in Badagry; offers 16+ junior secondary subjects.
evidence: Nigerian Schools Directory (erudites.ng) listing gives name, Badagry address, 2003 establishment date and photo.

name: Ansar-Ud-Deen Primary School, Ibonwon (Epe)
description: Public primary school established 10 September 1984, an Ansar-Ud-Deen mission school now in the Lagos state system.
address: Igboye Road, Ibonwon, Eredo, Epe, Lagos
evidence: Nigerian Schools Directory (erudites.ng) listing gives name, Igboye Road Ibonwon Epe address and 1984 establishment date.

name: Ansar-Ud-Deen Primary School, Odo-Egiri (Epe)
description: Public primary school in Odo-Egiri, Epe, bearing the Ansar-Ud-Deen Society name.
evidence: Nigerian Schools Directory (erudites.ng) listing gives name and 54 Yewa Road, Odo-Egiri, Epe address.

name: Ansar Ud Deen Academy, Surulere
description: Approved private secondary school in Surulere bearing the Ansar-Ud-Deen name; on the Lagos State OEQA approved private schools list (approved 2007).
address: 5 Owolewa Street, off Randle Avenue, Surulere, Lagos
evidence: Listed in Lagos State OEQA approved private secondary schools PDF with address and 2007 approval year.

name: Ansar-Ud-Deen Primary School, Alakoro (Lagos Island)
description: Public primary school on Lagos Island bearing the Ansar-Ud-Deen Society name.
evidence: Named in EduCeleb list of Lagos State public primary schools under Lagos Island LGA.

name: Ansar-Ud-Deen Primary School, Okepopo (Lagos Island)
description: Public primary school on Lagos Island bearing the Ansar-Ud-Deen Society name.
evidence: Named in EduCeleb list of Lagos State public primary schools under Lagos Island LGA.

name: Ansar-Ud-Deen Primary School, Isolo
description: Public primary school in Isolo bearing the Ansar-Ud-Deen Society name.
evidence: Named in EduCeleb list of Lagos State public primary schools under Oshodi-Isolo LGA.

name: Ansar-Ud-Deen Primary School, Igbogbo (Ikorodu)
description: Public primary school in Igbogbo, Ikorodu bearing the Ansar-Ud-Deen Society name.
evidence: Named in EduCeleb list of Lagos State public primary schools under Ikorodu LGA.

name: Anwar-Ul Islam Model College, Agege
description: Boys' college of the Anwar-Ul Islam Movement of Nigeria, established 5 April 1948 (as Saka Tinubu Memorial Ahmadiyya High School) — the first secondary school solely founded by Muslims in Nigeria; moved to Agege in 1956, renamed 1976; combines Islamic and Western education.
evidence: College's own website states Anwar-Ul Islam Movement ownership, 1948 founding history, Agege address, phone and email.

name: Anwar ul-Islam Girls High School, Ojokoro
description: Girls' junior and senior secondary school (day and boarding) in Ojokoro, historically of the Anwar-ul-Islam Movement (originally established 1954 as Ahmadiyya Grade III Teacher Training College; girls' high school founded 4 October 1972).
evidence: Wikipedia's List of schools in Lagos lists 'Anwar ul-Islam Girls High School — Ifako/Ijaye, Ojokoro Lagos'; founding details from Wikipedia/Wikidata search results.

name: Anwar-Ul-Islam Primary School, Agege
description: Public primary school in Agege bearing the Anwar-Ul-Islam Movement name.
evidence: Named in EduCeleb list of Lagos State public primary schools under Agege LGA.

name: Anwar-Ul-Islam Girls Primary School, Olushi (Lagos Island)
description: Public girls' primary school at Olushi, Lagos Island, bearing the Anwar-Ul-Islam Movement name (Olushi was the movement's original school site from 1948).
evidence: Named in EduCeleb list of Lagos State public primary schools under Lagos Island LGA.

name: Anwar-Ul-Islam Primary School, Ijede (Ikorodu)
description: Public primary school in Ijede, Ikorodu, bearing the Anwar-Ul-Islam Movement name.
evidence: Named in EduCeleb list of Lagos State public primary schools under Ikorodu LGA.

name: Talim-ul-Islam Ahmadiyya School, Aroloya (Lagos Island)
description: Established 4 September 1922 by the Ahmadiyya Muslim Community — the first Muslim school in Nigeria; the source states it still exists to date.
evidence: The Defender article on Ahmadiyya Muslim Jama'at contributions states the school's 1922 founding, Aroloya Street address and continued existence.

name: Talim-Ul-Islam Ahmadiyya Primary School, Ebute Metta
description: Public primary school in Ebute Metta (Lagos Mainland) bearing the Ahmadiyya Talim-ul-Islam name.
evidence: Named in EduCeleb list of Lagos State public primary schools under Mainland LGA.

name: Ahmadiyya Primary School, Iwaya
description: Public primary school in Iwaya (Lagos Mainland) of Ahmadiyya mission origin.
evidence: Named in EduCeleb list of Lagos State public primary schools under Mainland LGA.

name: Fazil-I-Omar Ahmadiyya Primary School, Okesuna (Lagos Island)
description: Public primary school at Okesuna, Lagos Island; Fazl-i-Omar schools were established by the Ahmadiyya Muslim Jama'at and later taken over by state governments.
evidence: Named in EduCeleb list of Lagos State public primary schools under Lagos Island LGA; Ahmadiyya origin of Fazl-i-Omar schools per The Muslim Times article.

name: Fazil Omar Ahmadiyya Primary School I, Ojo
description: Public primary school in Ojo of Ahmadiyya (Fazl-i-Omar) mission origin; a Fazil Omar Nursery and Primary School in Ojo joined the GLOBE science-education programme in 2020.
evidence: Named in EduCeleb list of Lagos State public primary schools under Ojo LGA (schools I, II and III listed); GLOBE.gov page confirms a Fazil Omar school in Ojo.

name: Fazil Omar Ahmadiya Primary School, Idado (Ibeju-Lekki)
description: Public primary school at Idado, Ibeju-Lekki, of Ahmadiyya (Fazl-i-Omar) mission origin.
evidence: Named in EduCeleb list of Lagos State public primary schools under Ibeju-Lekki LGA.

name: Mahmud Ahmadiyya College, Ijede (Ikorodu)
description: Private co-educational boarding secondary school established May 1994 by the Fazl-i-Omar Ahmadiyya Muslim Educational Foundation (FAME), a group of Ahmadi Muslims; emphasises Western, Islamic and Arabic education; government approved.
address: 101 Ikorodu Road, Abule-Eko, Ijede, Ikorodu, Lagos
evidence: College About page states 1994 founding by FAME (Ahmadi Muslims) at Abule Eko, Ijede; address and 2007 approval also appear on the Lagos OEQA approved private schools list PDF.

name: Zumratul Islamiyyah Primary School, Lagos Island
description: Public primary school of the Zumratul Islamiyyah society on Lagos Island.
address: 2 Tawaliu Bello, Ita-Akanni, Lagos Island, Lagos
evidence: Lagos Island public schools directory page gives name, 2 Tawaliu Bello Ita-Akanni address and phone number.

name: Zumratul Islamiyyah Junior Grammar School, Igbogbo (Ikorodu)
description: Public junior/senior grammar school in Igbogbo, Ikorodu, bearing the Zumratul Islamiyyah society name; its 2004 alumni set donated 30 benches and tables in 2024.
evidence: The Impact newspaper article covers the school (ZIGS) at Igbogbo, Ikorodu and its alumni donation.

name: Zumratul Islamiyyah Junior High School, Surulere
description: Public junior secondary school in Surulere bearing the Zumratul Islamiyyah society name.
evidence: MySpotFinder schools listing places the public junior secondary school in Mosafejo Ojuelegba Ward, Surulere.

name: Zumratul Islamiyyah Primary School I, Alaba (Ajeromi-Ifelodun)
description: Public primary school at Alaba, Ajeromi-Ifelodun, bearing the Zumratul Islamiyyah society name.
evidence: Named in EduCeleb list of Lagos State public primary schools under Ajeromi-Ifelodun LGA.

name: Zumratul Islamiyyah Primary School, Odogunyan (Ikorodu)
description: Public primary school at Odogunyan, Ikorodu, bearing the Zumratul Islamiyyah society name.
evidence: Named in EduCeleb list of Lagos State public primary schools under Ikorodu LGA.

name: Jamat-Ul-Islamiyya Primary School, Lagos Island
description: Public primary school on Lagos Island bearing the name of the Jamat-ul-Islamiyya society (founded 1924 by L.B. Agusto).
evidence: Named in EduCeleb list of Lagos State public primary schools under Lagos Island LGA.

name: Jamaatul Islamiyah Primary School, Sabo Ikorodu
description: Public primary school at Sabo, Ikorodu, bearing the Jamat-ul-Islamiyya name.
evidence: Named in EduCeleb list of Lagos State public primary schools under Ikorodu LGA.

name: MAN Nursery & Primary School, Surulere
description: Nursery and primary school of the Muslim Association of Nigeria (MAN), established 1983 with over 180 pupils; combines quality academic instruction with Islamic education.
address: 20 Amosu Street, off Bode-Thomas Street, Surulere, Lagos
evidence: Muslim Association of Nigeria official site 'Our Schools' page describes the school, its 1983 establishment and the Surulere address (site's phone/email are template placeholders, omitted).

name: MAN Secondary School, Surulere
description: Secondary school of the Muslim Association of Nigeria (MAN) emphasising academic excellence, character development, Islamic values, leadership and science and technology education.
address: 20 Amosu Street, off Bode-Thomas Street, Surulere, Lagos
evidence: Muslim Association of Nigeria official site 'Our Schools' page describes the secondary school at the association's Surulere address.

name: NUDS Zonal Comprehensive School, Owutu (Ikorodu)
description: Comprehensive school of the Nawair-Ud-Deen Society of Nigeria (Lagos Zone) comprising kindergarten, nursery, primary, junior and senior secondary sections; a N70m completion project reported as part of the society's N120m development drive. The society runs about 15 schools across Lagos State.
evidence: Guardian Nigeria article on NUDS Lagos Zone projects names the school, its Owutu Ikorodu location and its levels.

name: Lagos Muslim International College
description: Private Muslim school group (pre-school, primary and secondary college) established September 2016 by a group of Muslim professionals to provide quality Western and Islamic education; on the Lagos OEQA approved private secondary schools list (2021).
address: 17 Sadatu Street, off Lawal Street, by Clem, Jankara, Ijaiye-Ojokoro, Lagos (college); 73 Clem Road, Jankara (primary); 23 Raliatu Adepeju Street, Jankara (pre-school)
evidence: School's own website gives addresses, phones, emails and 2016 founding; also on Lagos OEQA approved list (Ifako-Ijaiye, 2021).

name: Thaqafatu Islamiyyah College, Badagry
description: Approved private secondary school with an Islamic-society name, on the Lagos State OEQA approved private schools list (approved 2020).
address: 1/3 Thaqafatul Islamiyyah Street, Mebamu New Layout (Nica Bus Stop), Badagry Expressway, Badagry, Lagos
evidence: Listed in Lagos State OEQA approved private secondary schools PDF with Badagry address and 2020 approval; association affiliation inferred from name only.

name: Ahmad Memorial Moslem Primary School, Oke-Koto (Agege)
description: Public primary school at Oke-Koto, Agege; an Ahmed Memorial Primary School in Agege is recorded as established by the late S. A. Ahmed in the Anwar-ul-Islam (Ahmadiyya) college community history.
evidence: Named in EduCeleb list of Lagos State public primary schools under Agege LGA; Ahmadiyya-community link noted in ACAOSA college history page.
